description_long: This house features a long rectangular footprint with a prominent orange gable roof that extends the full length of the structure. The front facade includes a covered porch area with a transparent awning, visible potted plants, and a wooden railing, suggesting a welcoming entryway. The exterior walls are finished in a light color, likely white or light gray, and the roof is clad in textured tiles. Windows are present beneath the awning, providing natural light to the front rooms, while the sides and rear of the house are largely unadorned and windowless. The overall design is simple and functional, with a single story and a focus on the front-facing porch. The house appears to be constructed primarily from masonry or concrete, with glass and metal elements in the awning and windows.
description: A single-story rectangular house with an orange gable roof, a covered front porch with a transparent awning, and light-colored exterior walls. The design is simple, with most architectural detail focused on the front entryway.
description_view_0: Front view showing the gable roof, covered porch with transparent awning, potted plants, and wooden railing.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the long orange roof, plain light-colored wall, and the edge of the front awning.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the plain gable end of the roof and unadorned back wall.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the length of the roof and the side wall.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement, concentrated at the front under the awning
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway features a covered porch with a transparent awning, wooden railing, and potted plants, creating a welcoming and sheltered entrance.
